text:
Here’s what else we are covering:
Oil prices: The price of oil has risen steeply since the United States and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28, even as governments around the world — including the United States, which eased sanctions on some Russian oil — have sought to calm the volatility and increase supplies. International oil prices have continued to push higher, rising past $106 a barrel on Sunday, up more than 3 percent in early trading. Still, the move is more muted than some investors were expecting after news over the weekend of bombing in Iran encroaching on oil sites, and is not as high as some spikes seen last week.
Death toll: At least 1,348 civilians in Iran have been killed since the start of the war, Iran’s U.N. representative told the Security Council on Wednesday, the latest figure the country has provided. In Lebanon, officials said that 850 people had been killed. And in Israel at least 12 people have been killed, according to the authorities.
title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.
